parts.text:
so i want to build an app with the following features, it is going to be built with a golang back-end api.
vanilla js for the client.
and sqlite for the db.
i also have a gemini API key to use the AI.
i have written some technical checkpoints for our team to work on.
FEATURES:
-auth
-users with body metrics, preferences and monthly budget.
-user to be able to change the preferences and the budget.
-view daily/weekly/monthly program:
    (3 meals per day + snacks
    so the front end knows that it is going to be split in 4 sections per day.)
    view all the ingredients used with quantities and macros, view total macros.
-Take care of pantry:
  search ingredients from db, add in pantry WITH quantity.
  remove ingredients from pantry.
-check food made and consumed to remove ingredients from pantry automatically.
-Have a conversation in a chat interface and request a meal, request day food, request weekly program, request monthly program with buttons.
-Accept(store) program generated, decline(regenerate).
(optional if we have time)
-go to day and select, and cahnge the food.
TECHNICAL CHECKPOINTS:
-Make the auth system with the users stored in db with metrics and preferences.
-edit the preferences and budget.
-create DB schema for the recipes with ingredients and macros, and for the pantry.
-Import dataset for ingredients into a database. 
-fake seed those.
-view the recipes in a calendar like way per week.
-calculate the macros.
-calculate the cost.(the cost is going to come from the AI for the hackathon).
-pantry DB schema.
-add items, remove items, add purchases for the bugdet count.
-check recipe as done and consumed to remove from pantry.
-chat interface with AI. create meal button, create week meal plan, create monthly meal plan, accept(an incorporate into the users plan in the calendar), add to favorites but not in the plan.
-AI context management and tools or rag.
-match ingredients from ai with db.
-count macros.
(this is optional)
-make supermarket list based on pantry items.
take all of these insights and build a tasks.md which will have all the task in order for us to be able to create a short demo of this for a 8 hour hackathon.
do this in @tasks/TASKS.md 
after this, seperate md feiles are going to be generated for different sessions like this
Session A — Product Manager
Handles:
roadmap
priorities
specs
integration coordination
Session B — Backend
Session C — Frontend
Session D — AI/RAG
Session E — QA
every one of them is going to have a different task.md like this :
TASKS.md                 <- master coordination
/tasks
    backend.md
    frontend.md
    ai.md
    infra.md
    bugs.md
go head and write the TASKS.md first. 



so this was the previous prompt i just wanted to add it to this session so that entire has it. go head and review it and tell me what you think

metadata.preview:
# PantryPal Hackathon Plan (8 Hours)

This is the master coordination board for a short demo build.

## Demo Goal

Ship a working end-to-end flow where a user can:
1. Sign up/login.
2. Set body metrics, food preferences, and monthly budget.
3. Generate a daily/weekly meal plan with AI.
4. View meals in a calendar-style UI with macros and ingredient quantities.
5. Accept a generated plan and store it.
6. Manage pantry items and mark meals as consumed to auto-deduct pantry stock.

## Scope Guardrails (Hackathon)

- Prioritize **working happy paths** over edge-case completeness.
- Build daily + weekly plan first; monthly can reuse same pipeline.
- Keep auth simple (email/password + session/JWT).
- Use SQLite locally; no production deployment requirements.

state.title: tasks/TASKS.md

parts.text:
Great foundation — this is already very hackathon-friendly and execution-ready.

What I like most in `tasks/TASKS.md`:
- Clear demo goal with concrete user journey (`tasks/TASKS.md:5`).
- Good scope control for 8 hours (happy-path focus, simple auth, local SQLite) (`tasks/TASKS.md:15`).
- Strong phase sequencing from schema/auth -> pantry/macros -> AI -> UI -> stabilization (`tasks/TASKS.md:31`).
- Nice P0/P1/P2 prioritization and explicit “Definition of Done” (`tasks/TASKS.md:23`, `tasks/TASKS.md:132`).
- Good handoff intent for role-based task files (`tasks/TASKS.md:148`).

What I’d tighten before execution:
- Session/file mapping is slightly inconsistent: you mention Session A PM, B Backend, C Frontend, D AI/RAG, E QA, but planned files are `backend.md`, `frontend.md`, `ai.md`, `infra.md`, `bugs.md` (`tasks/TASKS.md:150`). Consider explicitly mapping PM->`infra.md` (or rename to `pm.md`) and QA->`bugs.md` to avoid confusion.
- Add one explicit “API contract freeze” checkpoint by ~2h so frontend/AI can build against stable endpoints.
- Add one fallback rule for AI outages (e.g., canned seeded plan) so the demo still works if Gemini latency/errors hit.
- Add minimal non-functional guardrails: one line for auth security basics (password hashing, token expiry), one line for SQLite migration strategy (single command for clean setup).
- “chat_messages” table is good, but specify whether storing full prompt/response is required for demo or optional to save time.

If you want, next I can propose a **clean v2 structure** for the five session files (backend/frontend/ai/infra/bugs) with strict ownership boundaries and dependency order so the team can parallelize immediately.

 # PantryPal Hackathon Plan (8 Hours)
 
 This is the master coordination board for a short demo build.
 
 ## Demo Goal
 
 Ship a working end-to-end flow where a user can:
 1. Sign up/login.
 2. Set body metrics, food preferences, and monthly budget.
 3. Generate a daily/weekly meal plan with AI.
 4. View meals in a calendar-style UI with macros and ingredient quantities.
 5. Accept a generated plan and store it.
 6. Manage pantry items and mark meals as consumed to auto-deduct pantry stock.
 
 ## Scope Guardrails (Hackathon)
 
 - Prioritize **working happy paths** over edge-case completeness.
 - Build daily + weekly plan first; monthly can reuse same pipeline.
 - Keep auth simple (email/password + session/JWT).
 - Use SQLite locally; no production deployment requirements.
 - Cost values can come from AI output for demo purposes.

 ### Phase 4 - Frontend demo flows (5:30-7:00)

 ## Definition of Done for Demo

@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+# Session D - AI/RAG Tasks
+
+## Owner
+
+- AI Engineer (Gemini integration + output normalization)
+
+## Objective
+
+- Provide reliable meal generation through Gemini with deterministic output shape for backend/frontend use.
+
+## Ordered Task List
+
+### 1) Integration baseline (0:30-1:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Implement Gemini client wrapper with timeout/retry guards.
+- [ ] `P0` Configure API key loading via env variables.
+- [ ] `P0` Define request templates for:
+  - single meal
+  - day plan
+  - week plan
+  - month plan
+
+### 2) Output schema and parser (1:30-3:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Define strict JSON schema for AI output.
+- [ ] `P0` Include required fields per meal:
+  - meal_type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack)
+  - recipe_name
+  - ingredient list with quantity and unit
+  - macros (protein, carbs, fat, calories)
+  - estimated cost
+- [ ] `P0` Build validation/parsing layer with clear error reasons.
+- [ ] `P0` Reject/repair malformed responses before backend persistence.
+
+### 3) Context and personalization (3:00-4:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Inject user context: preferences, body metrics, budget target, pantry snapshot.
+- [ ] `P0` Add constraints for 4-sections/day structure.
+- [ ] `P0` Add prompt guardrails for budget awareness and ingredient realism.
+- [ ] `P1` Add simple conversation memory window for follow-up prompts.
+
+### 4) Ingredient matching and macro consistency (4:30-5:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Build ingredient name normalization to map AI strings to DB ingredients.
+- [ ] `P0` Return unmatched ingredients list for backend/UI warnings.
+- [ ] `P0` Reconcile macro totals per meal/day/week in normalized output.
+- [ ] `P1` Add alias dictionary for common ingredient synonyms.
+
+### 5) Accept/decline and fallback mode (5:30-6:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Support regenerate flow with decline reason/context.
+- [ ] `P0` Ensure accepted proposal payload is stable for persistence.
+- [ ] `P0` Implement fallback canned generator if Gemini fails/timeouts.
+- [ ] `P0` Expose fallback status flag for frontend banner.
+
+### 6) Demo hardening (6:30-7:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Add observability fields (request id, model latency, parse status).
+- [ ] `P0` Prepare 2-3 tested prompt presets for live demo reliability.
+- [ ] `P1` Add favorites metadata output shape for optional feature.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- Backend: ingredient canonical list, plan proposal ingest schema, error contract.
+- Frontend: expected chat/proposal rendering shape.
+- PM: scope lock for personalization depth and month-plan expectations.
+- QA: failure-mode cases for malformed AI responses.
+
+## Risks
+
+- Non-deterministic model output breaking parser.
+- Ingredient mismatch rates too high for pantry deduction.
+- API rate limits/latency impacting live demo.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- AI output consistently validates against schema.
+- Plans generate for meal/day/week/month requests with 4 sections/day.
+- Ingredient mapping returns canonical IDs or explicit unmatched list.
+- Fallback generator can replace Gemini without breaking the demo path.

+# Session B - Backend Tasks
+
+## Owner
+
+- Backend Engineer (Go API + SQLite)
+
+## Objective
+
+- Deliver stable API and data layer for auth, profile, pantry, meal plans, macros, and consumption flows.
+
+## Ordered Task List
+
+### 1) Schema and setup first (0:30-1:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Implement SQLite schema + migrations for:
+  - users
+  - user_body_metrics
+  - user_preferences
+  - budgets
+  - purchases
+  - ingredients
+  - recipes
+  - recipe_ingredients
+  - pantry_items
+  - meal_plans
+  - plan_meals
+  - consumption_log
+  - chat_messages
+  - favorites (optional table behind feature flag/time)
+- [ ] `P0` Add one-command local DB setup/reset path.
+- [ ] `P0` Seed ingredients and minimal recipes for deterministic demo.
+
+### 2) Auth and profile APIs (1:30-2:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Auth endpoints: `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`, `GET /me`.
+- [ ] `P0` Password hashing and token/session expiry rules.
+- [ ] `P0` Profile endpoints for metrics, preferences, budget:
+  - `GET /profile`
+  - `PATCH /profile/metrics`
+  - `PATCH /profile/preferences`
+  - `PATCH /profile/budget`
+- [ ] `P0` Publish API v1 examples for frontend and AI sessions.
+
+### 3) Pantry and recipes APIs (2:30-3:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` `GET /ingredients/search?q=` for pantry add flow.
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /pantry/items` (ingredient + quantity + unit).
+- [ ] `P0` `PATCH /pantry/items/:id` (increment/decrement quantity).
+- [ ] `P0` `DELETE /pantry/items/:id`.
+- [ ] `P0` `GET /recipes/:id` with ingredient quantities and macros.
+- [ ] `P1` Purchases endpoints for budget tracking:
+  - `POST /purchases`
+  - `GET /budget/summary`
+
+### 4) Plan persistence and macro aggregation (3:30-5:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/proposal` ingestion endpoint for AI-normalized payload.
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/accept` persists to meal plans.
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/decline` archives/rejects and supports regeneration flow.
+- [ ] `P0` `GET /plans/week?start=` returns 7-day calendar payload.
+- [ ] `P0` Macro totals service in responses:
+  - per meal
+  - per day
+  - per week
+
+### 5) Consumption and pantry deduction (5:00-6:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plan-meals/:id/consume` marks consumed with timestamp.
+- [ ] `P0` Deduct pantry ingredients by recipe quantities.
+- [ ] `P0` Prevent negative pantry values (clamp at zero + warning field).
+- [ ] `P0` Log deductions to `consumption_log` for audit/debug.
+
+### 6) Hardening for demo (6:00-7:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Add consistent error format for frontend handling.
+- [ ] `P0` Add lightweight request logging and key action tracing.
+- [ ] `P0` Add smoke checks for auth -> plan accept -> consume flow.
+- [ ] `P1` Add favorite proposal endpoint if time remains.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- PM: frozen priority and final scope boundaries.
+- AI: normalized proposal JSON schema and ingredient matching policy.
+- Frontend: required response fields per screen.
+- QA: top-priority test scenarios and failure expectations.
+
+## Risks
+
+- AI names not matching ingredient table.
+- Unit mismatches (g/ml/piece) creating deduction inaccuracies.
+- Time overrun from building too many endpoints before core path is stable.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- API supports full happy path from auth to pantry auto-deduction.
+- Weekly plan endpoint returns 7-day, 4-section layout with macro totals.
+- DB setup/seed is reproducible in one command.
+- Frontend can run without backend contract guesswork.

+# Session E - QA Tasks
+
+## Owner
+
+- QA Engineer / Bug Triage Lead
+
+## Objective
+
+- Validate end-to-end happy path, catch critical failures early, and keep the demo stable.
+
+## Ordered Task List
+
+### 1) Test plan setup (0:30-1:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Create concise test checklist aligned to demo flow.
+- [ ] `P0` Define severity levels:
+  - `S0` demo blocker
+  - `S1` major workaround needed
+  - `S2` minor issue/cosmetic
+- [ ] `P0` Define bug report template (steps, expected, actual, env, screenshot/log).
+
+### 2) Early validation pass (1:00-3:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Validate auth flow: register, login, logout, bad credentials.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate profile updates: metrics, preferences, budget persistence.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate pantry flow: search/add/remove/decrement behavior.
+- [ ] `P0` Confirm API error messages are actionable for UI display.
+
+### 3) AI and plan flow validation (3:00-5:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Validate chat actions for meal/day/week/month generation.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate proposal structure: 4 sections/day and macros present.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate accept persists plan and appears in weekly calendar.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate decline triggers regenerate behavior.
+- [ ] `P1` Validate fallback mode when AI service fails.
+
+### 4) Consumption and data integrity checks (5:30-6:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Mark meal consumed and verify pantry deduction accuracy.
+- [ ] `P0` Verify no negative pantry values are shown.
+- [ ] `P0` Verify macro totals remain consistent after consumption actions.
+- [ ] `P1` Verify budget/purchase summary if implemented.
+
+### 5) Final regression and demo sign-off (6:30-8:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Execute full end-to-end regression script once before demo.
+- [ ] `P0` Re-test all fixed `S0`/`S1` bugs.
+- [ ] `P0` Produce final known-issues list with workarounds.
+- [ ] `P0` Sign-off checklist for PM with go/no-go recommendation.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- PM: canonical demo script and must-pass checkpoints.
+- Backend: stable test data/reset process and endpoint error contracts.
+- Frontend: expected UI states for loading/errors/empty data.
+- AI: expected fallback behavior and schema validation errors.
+
+## Risks
+
+- Late integration creates clustered `S0` defects.
+- Test data drift causes inconsistent reproducibility.
+- Fallback behavior untested until late, risking live demo failures.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- All `S0` issues are resolved or have accepted workaround paths.
+- End-to-end script passes in a clean environment.
+- Known issues and impact are documented for demo presenters.
+- QA sign-off delivered to PM before final demo run.

@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+# Session C - Frontend Tasks
+
+## Owner
+
+- Frontend Engineer (Vanilla JS)
+
+## Objective
+
+- Build a clean, fast demo UI that proves all core flows using the backend API.
+
+## Ordered Task List
+
+### 1) App shell and API client (0:30-1:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Set up page structure and shared layout (auth, profile, planner, pantry, chat).
+- [ ] `P0` Build API client helpers for auth headers, JSON handling, and error display.
+- [ ] `P0` Add simple route/state switching (no framework) between major sections.
+
+### 2) Auth and profile screens (1:30-2:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Register + login forms connected to API.
+- [ ] `P0` Persist auth token/session in browser storage for demo.
+- [ ] `P0` Profile form for body metrics, preferences, budget.
+- [ ] `P0` Save/update profile and show success/error feedback.
+
+### 3) Weekly planner UI (2:30-4:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Build 7-day calendar-like layout.
+- [ ] `P0` Render 4 sections per day: breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks.
+- [ ] `P0` Show ingredients, quantities, and macros per meal.
+- [ ] `P0` Show macro totals per day and per week.
+- [ ] `P1` Add lightweight monthly toggle view if backend payload is ready.
+
+### 4) Pantry and consumption flow (4:30-5:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Pantry ingredient search input with result list.
+- [ ] `P0` Add item with quantity/unit.
+- [ ] `P0` Increment/decrement/remove pantry items.
+- [ ] `P0` Add "consume meal" action in planner and refresh pantry state.
+
+### 5) Chat and plan actions (5:30-6:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Chat panel with action buttons:
+  - create meal
+  - create day plan
+  - create week plan
+  - create month plan
+- [ ] `P0` Render proposal preview returned by API.
+- [ ] `P0` Accept and decline buttons wired to proposal endpoints.
+- [ ] `P1` Favorite action button if endpoint is available.
+
+### 6) Demo polish and resilience (6:30-7:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Add loading and empty states for each screen.
+- [ ] `P0` Add fail-safe banner if AI service fallback mode is active.
+- [ ] `P0` Ensure mobile-usable layout and legible data density.
+- [ ] `P0` Prepare one-click path for live demo navigation.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- Backend: stable endpoint contracts and sample responses.
+- AI: proposal payload format and error/fallback response shape.
+- PM: final demo path and required screen order.
+- QA: bug priority list for final hour fixes.
+
+## Risks
+
+- Contract changes late in build causing UI rewiring.
+- Overbuilding visuals before core actions are connected.
+- Too much on one page reducing clarity in live demo.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- User can authenticate, edit profile, generate/accept a weekly plan, and consume a meal.
+- Planner clearly shows 7 days x 4 sections with macro totals.
+- Pantry updates reflect manual edits and automatic meal consumption.
+- Chat actions are usable via buttons without manual API calls.

+- [ ] `P0` Profile endpoints for metrics, preferences, budget:
+  - `GET /profile`
+  - `PATCH /profile/metrics`
+  - `PATCH /profile/preferences`
+  - `PATCH /profile/budget`
+- [ ] `P0` Publish API v1 examples for frontend and AI sessions.
+
+### 3) Pantry and recipes APIs (2:30-3:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` `GET /ingredients/search?q=` for pantry add flow.
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /pantry/items` (ingredient + quantity + unit).
+- [ ] `P0` `PATCH /pantry/items/:id` (increment/decrement quantity).
+- [ ] `P0` `DELETE /pantry/items/:id`.
+- [ ] `P0` `GET /recipes/:id` with ingredient quantities and macros.
+- [ ] `P1` Purchases endpoints for budget tracking:
+  - `POST /purchases`
+  - `GET /budget/summary`
+
+### 4) Plan persistence and macro aggregation (3:30-5:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/proposal` ingestion endpoint for AI-normalized payload.
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/accept` persists to meal plans.
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/decline` archives/rejects and supports regeneration flow.
+- [ ] `P0` `GET /plans/week?start=` returns 7-day calendar payload.
+- [ ] `P0` Macro totals service in responses:
+  - per meal
+  - per day
+  - per week
+
+### 5) Consumption and pantry deduction (5:00-6:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` `POST /plan-meals/:id/consume` marks consumed with timestamp.
+- [ ] `P0` Deduct pantry ingredients by recipe quantities.
+- [ ] `P0` Prevent negative pantry values (clamp at zero + warning field).
+- [ ] `P0` Log deductions to `consumption_log` for audit/debug.
+
+### 6) Hardening for demo (6:00-7:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Add consistent error format for frontend handling.
+- [ ] `P0` Add lightweight request logging and key action tracing.
+- [ ] `P0` Add smoke checks for auth -> plan accept -> consume flow.
+- [ ] `P1` Add favorite proposal endpoint if time remains.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- PM: frozen priority and final scope boundaries.
+- AI: normalized proposal JSON schema and ingredient matching policy.
+- Frontend: required response fields per screen.
+- QA: top-priority test scenarios and failure expectations.
+
+## Risks
+
+- AI names not matching ingredient table.
+- Unit mismatches (g/ml/piece) creating deduction inaccuracies.
+- Time overrun from building too many endpoints before core path is stable.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- API supports full happy path from auth to pantry auto-deduction.
+- Weekly plan endpoint returns 7-day, 4-section layout with macro totals.
+- DB setup/seed is reproducible in one command.
+- Frontend can run without backend contract guesswork.
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /root/Panathenaia/pantryPal/tasks/frontend.md
+# Session C - Frontend Tasks
+
+## Owner
+
+- Frontend Engineer (Vanilla JS)
+
+## Objective
+
+- Build a clean, fast demo UI that proves all core flows using the backend API.
+
+## Ordered Task List
+
+### 1) App shell and API client (0:30-1:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Set up page structure and shared layout (auth, profile, planner, pantry, chat).
+- [ ] `P0` Build API client helpers for auth headers, JSON handling, and error display.
+- [ ] `P0` Add simple route/state switching (no framework) between major sections.
+
+### 2) Auth and profile screens (1:30-2:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Register + login forms connected to API.
+- [ ] `P0` Persist auth token/session in browser storage for demo.
+- [ ] `P0` Profile form for body metrics, preferences, budget.
+- [ ] `P0` Save/update profile and show success/error feedback.
+
+### 3) Weekly planner UI (2:30-4:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Build 7-day calendar-like layout.
+- [ ] `P0` Render 4 sections per day: breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks.
+- [ ] `P0` Show ingredients, quantities, and macros per meal.
+- [ ] `P0` Show macro totals per day and per week.
+- [ ] `P1` Add lightweight monthly toggle view if backend payload is ready.
+
+### 4) Pantry and consumption flow (4:30-5:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Pantry ingredient search input with result list.
+- [ ] `P0` Add item with quantity/unit.
+- [ ] `P0` Increment/decrement/remove pantry items.
+- [ ] `P0` Add "consume meal" action in planner and refresh pantry state.
+
+### 5) Chat and plan actions (5:30-6:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Chat panel with action buttons:
+  - create meal
+  - create day plan
+  - create week plan
+  - create month plan
+- [ ] `P0` Render proposal preview returned by API.
+- [ ] `P0` Accept and decline buttons wired to proposal endpoints.
+- [ ] `P1` Favorite action button if endpoint is available.
+
+### 6) Demo polish and resilience (6:30-7:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Add loading and empty states for each screen.
+- [ ] `P0` Add fail-safe banner if AI service fallback mode is active.
+- [ ] `P0` Ensure mobile-usable layout and legible data density.
+- [ ] `P0` Prepare one-click path for live demo navigation.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- Backend: stable endpoint contracts and sample responses.
+- AI: proposal payload format and error/fallback response shape.
+- PM: final demo path and required screen order.
+- QA: bug priority list for final hour fixes.
+
+## Risks
+
+- Contract changes late in build causing UI rewiring.
+- Overbuilding visuals before core actions are connected.
+- Too much on one page reducing clarity in live demo.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- User can authenticate, edit profile, generate/accept a weekly plan, and consume a meal.
+- Planner clearly shows 7 days x 4 sections with macro totals.
+- Pantry updates reflect manual edits and automatic meal consumption.
+- Chat actions are usable via buttons without manual API calls.
*** End Patch

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /root/Panathenaia/pantryPal/tasks/ai.md
+# Session D - AI/RAG Tasks
+
+## Owner
+
+- AI Engineer (Gemini integration + output normalization)
+
+## Objective
+
+- Provide reliable meal generation through Gemini with deterministic output shape for backend/frontend use.
+
+## Ordered Task List
+
+### 1) Integration baseline (0:30-1:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Implement Gemini client wrapper with timeout/retry guards.
+- [ ] `P0` Configure API key loading via env variables.
+- [ ] `P0` Define request templates for:
+  - single meal
+  - day plan
+  - week plan
+  - month plan
+
+### 2) Output schema and parser (1:30-3:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Define strict JSON schema for AI output.
+- [ ] `P0` Include required fields per meal:
+  - meal_type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack)
+  - recipe_name
+  - ingredient list with quantity and unit
+  - macros (protein, carbs, fat, calories)
+  - estimated cost
+- [ ] `P0` Build validation/parsing layer with clear error reasons.
+- [ ] `P0` Reject/repair malformed responses before backend persistence.
+
+### 3) Context and personalization (3:00-4:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Inject user context: preferences, body metrics, budget target, pantry snapshot.
+- [ ] `P0` Add constraints for 4-sections/day structure.
+- [ ] `P0` Add prompt guardrails for budget awareness and ingredient realism.
+- [ ] `P1` Add simple conversation memory window for follow-up prompts.
+
+### 4) Ingredient matching and macro consistency (4:30-5:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Build ingredient name normalization to map AI strings to DB ingredients.
+- [ ] `P0` Return unmatched ingredients list for backend/UI warnings.
+- [ ] `P0` Reconcile macro totals per meal/day/week in normalized output.
+- [ ] `P1` Add alias dictionary for common ingredient synonyms.
+
+### 5) Accept/decline and fallback mode (5:30-6:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Support regenerate flow with decline reason/context.
+- [ ] `P0` Ensure accepted proposal payload is stable for persistence.
+- [ ] `P0` Implement fallback canned generator if Gemini fails/timeouts.
+- [ ] `P0` Expose fallback status flag for frontend banner.
+
+### 6) Demo hardening (6:30-7:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Add observability fields (request id, model latency, parse status).
+- [ ] `P0` Prepare 2-3 tested prompt presets for live demo reliability.
+- [ ] `P1` Add favorites metadata output shape for optional feature.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- Backend: ingredient canonical list, plan proposal ingest schema, error contract.
+- Frontend: expected chat/proposal rendering shape.
+- PM: scope lock for personalization depth and month-plan expectations.
+- QA: failure-mode cases for malformed AI responses.
+
+## Risks
+
+- Non-deterministic model output breaking parser.
+- Ingredient mismatch rates too high for pantry deduction.
+- API rate limits/latency impacting live demo.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- AI output consistently validates against schema.
+- Plans generate for meal/day/week/month requests with 4 sections/day.
+- Ingredient mapping returns canonical IDs or explicit unmatched list.
+- Fallback generator can replace Gemini without breaking the demo path.
*** End Patch

+  - meal_type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack)
+  - recipe_name
+  - ingredient list with quantity and unit
+  - macros (protein, carbs, fat, calories)
+  - estimated cost
+- [ ] `P0` Build validation/parsing layer with clear error reasons.
+- [ ] `P0` Reject/repair malformed responses before backend persistence.
+
+### 3) Context and personalization (3:00-4:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Inject user context: preferences, body metrics, budget target, pantry snapshot.
+- [ ] `P0` Add constraints for 4-sections/day structure.
+- [ ] `P0` Add prompt guardrails for budget awareness and ingredient realism.
+- [ ] `P1` Add simple conversation memory window for follow-up prompts.
+
+### 4) Ingredient matching and macro consistency (4:30-5:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Build ingredient name normalization to map AI strings to DB ingredients.
+- [ ] `P0` Return unmatched ingredients list for backend/UI warnings.
+- [ ] `P0` Reconcile macro totals per meal/day/week in normalized output.
+- [ ] `P1` Add alias dictionary for common ingredient synonyms.
+
+### 5) Accept/decline and fallback mode (5:30-6:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Support regenerate flow with decline reason/context.
+- [ ] `P0` Ensure accepted proposal payload is stable for persistence.
+- [ ] `P0` Implement fallback canned generator if Gemini fails/timeouts.
+- [ ] `P0` Expose fallback status flag for frontend banner.
+
+### 6) Demo hardening (6:30-7:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Add observability fields (request id, model latency, parse status).
+- [ ] `P0` Prepare 2-3 tested prompt presets for live demo reliability.
+- [ ] `P1` Add favorites metadata output shape for optional feature.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- Backend: ingredient canonical list, plan proposal ingest schema, error contract.
+- Frontend: expected chat/proposal rendering shape.
+- PM: scope lock for personalization depth and month-plan expectations.
+- QA: failure-mode cases for malformed AI responses.
+
+## Risks
+
+- Non-deterministic model output breaking parser.
+- Ingredient mismatch rates too high for pantry deduction.
+- API rate limits/latency impacting live demo.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- AI output consistently validates against schema.
+- Plans generate for meal/day/week/month requests with 4 sections/day.
+- Ingredient mapping returns canonical IDs or explicit unmatched list.
+- Fallback generator can replace Gemini without breaking the demo path.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /root/Panathenaia/pantryPal/tasks/bugs.md
+# Session E - QA Tasks
+
+## Owner
+
+- QA Engineer / Bug Triage Lead
+
+## Objective
+
+- Validate end-to-end happy path, catch critical failures early, and keep the demo stable.
+
+## Ordered Task List
+
+### 1) Test plan setup (0:30-1:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Create concise test checklist aligned to demo flow.
+- [ ] `P0` Define severity levels:
+  - `S0` demo blocker
+  - `S1` major workaround needed
+  - `S2` minor issue/cosmetic
+- [ ] `P0` Define bug report template (steps, expected, actual, env, screenshot/log).
+
+### 2) Early validation pass (1:00-3:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Validate auth flow: register, login, logout, bad credentials.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate profile updates: metrics, preferences, budget persistence.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate pantry flow: search/add/remove/decrement behavior.
+- [ ] `P0` Confirm API error messages are actionable for UI display.
+
+### 3) AI and plan flow validation (3:00-5:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Validate chat actions for meal/day/week/month generation.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate proposal structure: 4 sections/day and macros present.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate accept persists plan and appears in weekly calendar.
+- [ ] `P0` Validate decline triggers regenerate behavior.
+- [ ] `P1` Validate fallback mode when AI service fails.
+
+### 4) Consumption and data integrity checks (5:30-6:30)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Mark meal consumed and verify pantry deduction accuracy.
+- [ ] `P0` Verify no negative pantry values are shown.
+- [ ] `P0` Verify macro totals remain consistent after consumption actions.
+- [ ] `P1` Verify budget/purchase summary if implemented.
+
+### 5) Final regression and demo sign-off (6:30-8:00)
+
+- [ ] `P0` Execute full end-to-end regression script once before demo.
+- [ ] `P0` Re-test all fixed `S0`/`S1` bugs.
+- [ ] `P0` Produce final known-issues list with workarounds.
+- [ ] `P0` Sign-off checklist for PM with go/no-go recommendation.
+
+## Contracts Needed From Others
+
+- PM: canonical demo script and must-pass checkpoints.
+- Backend: stable test data/reset process and endpoint error contracts.
+- Frontend: expected UI states for loading/errors/empty data.
+- AI: expected fallback behavior and schema validation errors.
+
+## Risks
+
+- Late integration creates clustered `S0` defects.
+- Test data drift causes inconsistent reproducibility.
+- Fallback behavior untested until late, risking live demo failures.
+
+## Done Criteria
+
+- All `S0` issues are resolved or have accepted workaround paths.
+- End-to-end script passes in a clean environment.
+- Known issues and impact are documented for demo presenters.
+- QA sign-off delivered to PM before final demo run.
*** End Patch

<content>
1: # PantryPal Hackathon Plan (8 Hours)
2: 
3: This is the master coordination board for a short demo build.
4: 
5: ## Demo Goal
6: 
7: Ship a working end-to-end flow where a user can:
8: 1. Sign up/login.
9: 2. Set body metrics, food preferences, and monthly budget.
10: 3. Generate a daily/weekly meal plan with AI.
11: 4. View meals in a calendar-style UI with macros and ingredient quantities.
12: 5. Accept a generated plan and store it.
13: 6. Manage pantry items and mark meals as consumed to auto-deduct pantry stock.
14: 
15: ## Scope Guardrails (Hackathon)
16: 
17: - Prioritize **working happy paths** over edge-case completeness.
18: - Build daily + weekly plan first; monthly can reuse same pipeline.
19: - Keep auth simple (email/password + session/JWT).
20: - Use SQLite locally; no production deployment requirements.
21: - Cost values can come from AI output for demo purposes.
22: 
23: ## Priorities
24: 
25: - `P0` = must have for demo.
26: - `P1` = strong add if time permits.
27: - `P2` = optional stretch.
28: 
29: ## Master Task Order
30: 
31: ### Phase 0 - Project setup (0:00-0:30)
32: 
33: - [ ] `P0` Confirm repo structure and ownership for sessions:
34:   - `/tasks/TASKS.md` (this file)
35:   - `/tasks/backend.md`
36:   - `/tasks/frontend.md`
37:   - `/tasks/ai.md`
38:   - `/tasks/infra.md`
39:   - `/tasks/bugs.md`
40: - [ ] `P0` Define API contract skeleton (auth, profile, pantry, plans, chat).
41: - [ ] `P0` Define auth/security minimums (password hashing, token expiry).
42: - [ ] `P0` Decide timebox checkpoints at 2h / 4h / 6h / 8h.
43: 
44: ### Phase 1 - Data model and auth foundation (0:30-2:00)
45: 
46: - [ ] `P0` Create SQLite schema + migrations for:
47:   - users
48:   - user_preferences
49:   - user_body_metrics
50:   - budgets / purchases
51:   - ingredients (seed dataset)
52:   - recipes + recipe_ingredients
53:   - pantry_items
54:   - meal_plans (day/week/month)
55:   - plan_meals
56:   - consumption_log
57:   - chat_messages
58: - [ ] `P0` Provide one-command DB setup/reset for all sessions.
59: - [ ] `P0` Seed ingredient data (fake seed acceptable).
60: - [ ] `P0` Implement auth endpoints:
61:   - register
62:   - login
63:   - current user (`/me`)
64: - [ ] `P0` Implement profile endpoints to read/update:
65:   - body metrics
66:   - preferences
67:   - monthly budget
68: - [ ] `P0` Freeze API v1 contract at 2h mark so frontend/AI can continue in parallel.
69: 
70: ### Phase 2 - Pantry + recipes + macros pipeline (2:00-3:30)
71: 
72: - [ ] `P0` Pantry endpoints:
73:   - search ingredients
74:   - add item with quantity
75:   - remove item / decrement quantity
76: - [ ] `P0` Recipe read endpoints with ingredients + macros.
77: - [ ] `P0` Macro aggregation service:
78:   - per meal
79:   - per day
80:   - per week
81: - [ ] `P1` Budget tracking endpoint for purchases and current monthly spend.
82: 
83: ### Phase 3 - AI meal plan generation and acceptance (3:30-5:30)
84: 
85: - [ ] `P0` Integrate Gemini API service wrapper.
86: - [ ] `P0` Add fallback generator (seeded/canned plan) if Gemini is unavailable.
87: - [ ] `P0` Chat endpoint with actions:
88:   - request meal
89:   - request day plan
90:   - request week plan
91:   - request month plan
92: - [ ] `P0` Normalize AI output into internal plan format:
93:   - day split into 4 sections (breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks)
94:   - ingredient names mapped to DB ingredients
95:   - quantities, macros, and AI-provided cost captured
96: - [ ] `P0` Accept/decline flow:
97:   - decline => regenerate proposal
98:   - accept => persist to meal_plans + plan_meals
99: - [ ] `P1` Add favorite action (store proposal without scheduling).
100: - [ ] `P1` Decide chat retention for demo (store full messages vs summary metadata).
101: 
102: ### Phase 4 - Frontend demo flows (5:30-7:00)
103: 
104: - [ ] `P0` Vanilla JS auth screens (register/login/logout).
105: - [ ] `P0` Profile screen for metrics, preferences, budget editing.
106: - [ ] `P0` Calendar-like weekly view with:
107:   - 7 days
108:   - 4 meal sections/day
109:   - total macros/day and week
110: - [ ] `P0` Pantry management UI:
111:   - ingredient search
112:   - add/remove quantity
113: - [ ] `P0` Chat interface with action buttons:
114:   - create meal
115:   - create day plan
116:   - create week plan
117:   - create month plan
118:   - accept / decline
119: - [ ] `P0` Mark meal consumed action triggers pantry deduction.
120: 
121: ### Phase 5 - Stabilize, test, and demo prep (7:00-8:00)
122: 
123: - [ ] `P0` End-to-end happy-path test script:
124:   - sign up
125:   - set profile
126:   - generate week plan
127:   - accept plan
128:   - view macros
129:   - consume one meal
130:   - verify pantry deduction
131: - [ ] `P0` Fix top-priority bugs and broken UI/API links.
132: - [ ] `P0` Prepare demo data for one realistic user journey.
133: - [ ] `P1` Add monthly view polish.
134: - [ ] `P2` Optional: change one meal on a selected day.
135: - [ ] `P2` Optional: supermarket list from pantry gaps.
136: 
137: ## Definition of Done for Demo
138: 
139: - User can authenticate and update profile (metrics/preferences/budget).
140: - User can generate and accept a weekly plan from chat.
141: - Weekly view shows meals and macro totals.
142: - Pantry can be edited manually and auto-deducts on meal consumption.
143: - System runs locally with SQLite and seeded data.
144: 
145: ## Session Ownership Map
146: 
147: - Session A (Product Manager): roadmap, priorities, specs, integration coordination.
148: - Session B (Backend): DB schema, auth, pantry, plans, macros, consumption logic.
149: - Session C (Frontend): auth/profile UI, calendar views, pantry UI, chat UI.
150: - Session D (AI/RAG): Gemini integration, prompt format, output normalization, ingredient matching.
151: - Session E (QA): test checklist, bug triage, regression pass before demo.
152: 
153: ## Handoff Notes for Next Step
154: 
155: Next files to generate from this master:
156: - `/tasks/pm.md`
157: - `/tasks/backend.md`
158: - `/tasks/frontend.md`
159: - `/tasks/ai.md`
160: - `/tasks/bugs.md`
161: 
162: Each file should include:
163: - owner
164: - objective
165: - ordered task list
166: - API/UI contracts needed from others
167: - risk list
168: - done criteria

(End of file - total 168 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# PantryPal Hackathon Plan (8 Hours)

This is the master coordination board for a short demo build.

## Demo Goal

Ship a working end-to-end flow where a user can:
1. Sign up/login.
2. Set body metrics, food preferences, and monthly budget.
3. Generate a daily/weekly meal plan with AI.
4. View meals in a calendar-style UI with macros and ingredient quantities.
5. Accept a generated plan and store it.
6. Manage pantry items and mark meals as consumed to auto-deduct pantry stock.

## Scope Guardrails (Hackathon)

- Prioritize **working happy paths** over edge-case completeness.
- Build daily + weekly plan first; monthly can reuse same pipeline.
- Keep auth simple (email/password + session/JWT).
- Use SQLite locally; no production deployment requirements.

1: # Session A - Product Manager Tasks
2: 
3: ## Owner
4: 
5: - Product Manager / Integration Lead
6: 
7: ## Objective
8: 
9: - Keep the 8-hour demo focused on a working end-to-end flow.
10: - Lock scope quickly, unblock cross-session dependencies, and manage risk/time.
11: 
12: ## Ordered Task List
13: 
14: ### 0) Kickoff and scope lock (0:00-0:30)
15: 
16: - [ ] `P0` Confirm demo storyline: signup -> profile setup -> generate week plan -> accept -> pantry update -> consume meal.
17: - [ ] `P0` Confirm out-of-scope items for this sprint (full optimization, advanced settings, heavy polish).
18: - [ ] `P0` Assign session owners and communication cadence (check-ins at 2h/4h/6h/8h).
19: - [ ] `P0` Create shared decision log with timestamps and owner.
20: 
21: ### 1) Spec freeze for parallel work (0:30-2:00)
22: 
23: - [ ] `P0` Freeze API v1 contract by 2h mark.
24: - [ ] `P0` Freeze AI response shape v1 (meals/day split, ingredient fields, macros, cost).
25: - [ ] `P0` Freeze frontend MVP screens and navigation map.
26: - [ ] `P0` Define acceptance rules for plan proposal (accept, decline/regenerate, favorite optional).
27: 
28: ### 2) Integration management (2:00-6:30)
29: 
30: - [ ] `P0` Resolve cross-team blockers in <=15 minutes each.
31: - [ ] `P0` Verify backend/frontend contract alignment after every major endpoint.
32: - [ ] `P0` Verify AI ingredient matching strategy and fallback behavior.
33: - [ ] `P1` Keep release notes updated with what is demo-ready vs partially complete.
34: 
35: ### 3) Demo readiness (6:30-8:00)
36: 
37: - [ ] `P0` Run full walkthrough with QA observer.
38: - [ ] `P0` Prepare demo script with exact clicks and API actions.
39: - [ ] `P0` Mark risks and fallback moves for each step (especially AI outage).
40: - [ ] `P0` Final go/no-go checklist with owners for each section.
41: 
42: ## Contracts Needed From Others
43: 
44: - Backend: stable endpoint list, payload examples, migration/setup command.
45: - Frontend: final routes/screens and expected loading/error states.
46: - AI: prompt template, JSON schema, fallback plan output format.
47: - QA: final bug severity rubric and test checklist coverage.
48: 
49: ## Risks
50: 
51: - API contract drift after 2h causing rework.
52: - AI output variability breaking parser.
53: - Pantry deduction logic unclear at edge cases.
54: - Time spent on optional features reducing demo stability.
55: 
56: ## Done Criteria

Done — I added placeholder files so every architecture folder is trackable in GitHub.

I used `.gitkeep` files across all created directories, including:
- `backend/...` (all module/platform/transport/test/migration/seed dirs)
- `frontend/...` (public/assets/js/css subfolders)
- `ai/...` (`prompts`, `schemas`, `fixtures`)
- `database/sqlite`
- `docs/api`, `docs/architecture`
- `scripts/dev`, `scripts/db`, `scripts/seed`, `scripts/qa`

Example created file:
- `backend/internal/modules/auth/.gitkeep`

You can now `git add .` and push the architecture safely.

If you want, I can next replace a few `.gitkeep` files with minimal starter files (`backend/cmd/api/main.go`, `frontend/public/index.html`, `frontend/src/js/app.js`, `backend/migrations/0001_init.sql`) so teammates can start coding immediately.
have a look at the whole project and update the to do based on what is already implemented and what is not

 # PantryPal Hackathon Plan (8 Hours)
 
 This is the master coordination board for a short demo build.
 
+## Current Status Snapshot
+
+- Backend foundation is implemented for app bootstrap, auth, profile, token auth, DB migrations, demo seeds, and local DB reset scripts.
+- Backend docs exist for auth/profile API and DB setup.
+- Frontend is not implemented yet beyond folder scaffolding.
+- AI/RAG integration is not implemented yet beyond folder scaffolding.
+- QA automation/checklists are not implemented yet.
+
 ## Demo Goal
 
 Ship a working end-to-end flow where a user can:
 1. Sign up/login.
 2. Set body metrics, food preferences, and monthly budget.
 3. Generate a daily/weekly meal plan with AI.
 4. View meals in a calendar-style UI with macros and ingredient quantities.
 5. Accept a generated plan and store it.
 6. Manage pantry items and mark meals as consumed to auto-deduct pantry stock.
 
 ## Scope Guardrails (Hackathon)
 
 - Prioritize **working happy paths** over edge-case completeness.
 - Build daily + weekly plan first; monthly can reuse same pipeline.
 - Keep auth simple (email/password + session/JWT).
 - Use SQLite locally; no production deployment requirements.
 - Cost values can come from AI output for demo purposes.
 
 ## Priorities
 
 - `P0` = must have for demo.
 - `P1` = strong add if time permits.
 - `P2` = optional stretch.
 
 ## Master Task Order
 
 ### Phase 0 - Project setup (0:00-0:30)
 
-- [ ] `P0` Confirm repo structure and ownership for sessions:
+- [x] `P0` Confirm repo structure and ownership for sessions:

   - `/tasks/pm.md`
   - `/tasks/backend.md`
   - `/tasks/frontend.md`
   - `/tasks/ai.md`
   - `/tasks/bugs.md`
 - [ ] `P0` Define API contract skeleton (auth, profile, pantry, plans, chat).
-- [ ] `P0` Define auth/security minimums (password hashing, token expiry).
+- [x] `P0` Define auth/security minimums (password hashing, token expiry).
 - [ ] `P0` Decide timebox checkpoints at 2h / 4h / 6h / 8h.
 
 ### Phase 1 - Data model and auth foundation (0:30-2:00)
 
-- [ ] `P0` Create SQLite schema + migrations for:
+- [x] `P0` Create SQLite schema + migrations for:
   - users
   - user_preferences
   - user_body_metrics
   - budgets / purchases
-  - ingredients (seed dataset)
+  - USDA foods dataset tables

-- [ ] `P0` Implement auth endpoints:
+- [x] `P0` Provide one-command DB setup/reset for all sessions.
+- [x] `P0` Seed ingredient data and demo app data.
+- [x] `P0` Implement auth endpoints:

-- [ ] `P0` Implement profile endpoints to read/update:
+- [x] `P0` Implement profile endpoints to read/update:
   - body metrics

 - [ ] `P0` Freeze API v1 contract at 2h mark so frontend/AI can continue in parallel.
 
 ### Phase 2 - Pantry + recipes + macros pipeline (2:00-3:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Pantry endpoints:
   - search ingredients
   - add item with quantity
   - remove item / decrement quantity
 - [ ] `P0` Recipe read endpoints with ingredients + macros.
 - [ ] `P0` Macro aggregation service:
   - per meal
   - per day
   - per week
 - [ ] `P1` Budget tracking endpoint for purchases and current monthly spend.
 
 ### Phase 3 - AI meal plan generation and acceptance (3:30-5:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Integrate Gemini API service wrapper.
 - [ ] `P0` Add fallback generator (seeded/canned plan) if Gemini is unavailable.
 - [ ] `P0` Chat endpoint with actions:
   - request meal
   - request day plan
   - request week plan
   - request month plan
 - [ ] `P0` Normalize AI output into internal plan format:
   - day split into 4 sections (breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks)
   - ingredient names mapped to DB ingredients
   - quantities, macros, and AI-provided cost captured
 - [ ] `P0` Accept/decline flow:
   - decline => regenerate proposal
   - accept => persist to meal_plans + plan_meals
 - [ ] `P1` Add favorite action (store proposal without scheduling).
 - [ ] `P1` Decide chat retention for demo (store full messages vs summary metadata).
 
 ### Phase 4 - Frontend demo flows (5:30-7:00)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Vanilla JS auth screens (register/login/logout).
 - [ ] `P0` Profile screen for metrics, preferences, budget editing.
 - [ ] `P0` Calendar-like weekly view with:
   - 7 days
   - 4 meal sections/day
   - total macros/day and week
 - [ ] `P0` Pantry management UI:
   - ingredient search
   - add/remove quantity
 - [ ] `P0` Chat interface with action buttons:
   - create meal
   - create day plan
   - create week plan
   - create month plan
   - accept / decline
 - [ ] `P0` Mark meal consumed action triggers pantry deduction.
 
 ### Phase 5 - Stabilize, test, and demo prep (7:00-8:00)
 
 - [ ] `P0` End-to-end happy-path test script:
   - sign up
   - set profile
   - generate week plan
   - accept plan
   - view macros
   - consume one meal
   - verify pantry deduction
 - [ ] `P0` Fix top-priority bugs and broken UI/API links.
 - [ ] `P0` Prepare demo data for one realistic user journey.
 - [ ] `P1` Add monthly view polish.
 - [ ] `P2` Optional: change one meal on a selected day.
 - [ ] `P2` Optional: supermarket list from pantry gaps.
 
 ## Definition of Done for Demo
 
 - User can authenticate and update profile (metrics/preferences/budget).
 - User can generate and accept a weekly plan from chat.
 - Weekly view shows meals and macro totals.
 - Pantry can be edited manually and auto-deducts on meal consumption.
 - System runs locally with SQLite and seeded data.
 
+## Implemented Now
+
+- Go backend entrypoint and app wiring.
+- SQLite schema for core auth/profile/pantry/recipes/plans/chat data plus USDA food tables.
+- Demo seed SQL for one user, pantry items, recipes, and recipe ingredients.
+- Local DB bootstrap/reset scripts.
+- `GET /health`, `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`, `GET /me`.
+- `GET /profile`, `PATCH /profile/metrics`, `PATCH /profile/preferences`, `PATCH /profile/budget`.
+- Auth/profile API documentation.
+
+## Still Missing
+
+- Pantry search and CRUD endpoints/UI.
+- Recipe read endpoints and macro rollups.
+- Meal plan creation, proposal, accept/decline, and weekly view payloads.
+- Consumption logging endpoint and pantry auto-deduction logic.
+- Gemini integration, fallback planner, and chat interface.
+- Any frontend implementation.
+- QA checklist, regression script, and automated tests.
+
 ## Session Ownership Map
 
 - Session A (Product Manager): roadmap, priorities, specs, integration coordination.
 - Session B (Backend): DB schema, auth, pantry, plans, macros, consumption logic.
 - Session C (Frontend): auth/profile UI, calendar views, pantry UI, chat UI.
 - Session D (AI/RAG): Gemini integration, prompt format, output normalization, ingredient matching.
 - Session E (QA): test checklist, bug triage, regression pass before demo.
 
 ## Handoff Notes for Next Step
 
 Next files to generate from this master:
 - `/tasks/pm.md`
 - `/tasks/backend.md`
 - `/tasks/frontend.md`
 - `/tasks/ai.md`
 - `/tasks/bugs.md`
 
 Each file should include:
 - owner
 - objective
 - ordered task list
 - API/UI contracts needed from others
 - risk list
 - done criteria

 # Session D - AI/RAG Tasks
 
 ## Owner
 
 - AI Engineer (Gemini integration + output normalization)
 
 ## Objective
 
 - Provide reliable meal generation through Gemini with deterministic output shape for backend/frontend use.
 
+## Current Status Snapshot
+
+- AI directory is scaffolding only.
+- No Gemini client, prompt templates, schema validation, matching, or fallback implementation exists yet.
+- Backend already has schema tables ready for meal plans and chat persistence.
+
 ## Ordered Task List
 
 ### 1) Integration baseline (0:30-1:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Implement Gemini client wrapper with timeout/retry guards.
 - [ ] `P0` Configure API key loading via env variables.
 - [ ] `P0` Define request templates for:
   - single meal
   - day plan
   - week plan
   - month plan
 
 ### 2) Output schema and parser (1:30-3:00)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Define strict JSON schema for AI output.
 - [ ] `P0` Include required fields per meal:
   - meal_type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack)
   - recipe_name
   - ingredient list with quantity and unit
   - macros (protein, carbs, fat, calories)
   - estimated cost
 - [ ] `P0` Build validation/parsing layer with clear error reasons.
 - [ ] `P0` Reject/repair malformed responses before backend persistence.
 
 ### 3) Context and personalization (3:00-4:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Inject user context: preferences, body metrics, budget target, pantry snapshot.
 - [ ] `P0` Add constraints for 4-sections/day structure.
 - [ ] `P0` Add prompt guardrails for budget awareness and ingredient realism.
 - [ ] `P1` Add simple conversation memory window for follow-up prompts.
 
 ### 4) Ingredient matching and macro consistency (4:30-5:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Build ingredient name normalization to map AI strings to DB ingredients.
 - [ ] `P0` Return unmatched ingredients list for backend/UI warnings.
 - [ ] `P0` Reconcile macro totals per meal/day/week in normalized output.
 - [ ] `P1` Add alias dictionary for common ingredient synonyms.
 
 ### 5) Accept/decline and fallback mode (5:30-6:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Support regenerate flow with decline reason/context.
 - [ ] `P0` Ensure accepted proposal payload is stable for persistence.
 - [ ] `P0` Implement fallback canned generator if Gemini fails/timeouts.
 - [ ] `P0` Expose fallback status flag for frontend banner.
 
 ### 6) Demo hardening (6:30-7:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Add observability fields (request id, model latency, parse status).
 - [ ] `P0` Prepare 2-3 tested prompt presets for live demo reliability.
 - [ ] `P1` Add favorites metadata output shape for optional feature.
 
 ## Contracts Needed From Others
 
 - Backend: ingredient canonical list, plan proposal ingest schema, error contract.
 - Frontend: expected chat/proposal rendering shape.
 - PM: scope lock for personalization depth and month-plan expectations.
 - QA: failure-mode cases for malformed AI responses.
 
 ## Risks
 
 - Non-deterministic model output breaking parser.
 - Ingredient mismatch rates too high for pantry deduction.
 - API rate limits/latency impacting live demo.
 
 ## Done Criteria
 
 - AI output consistently validates against schema.
 - Plans generate for meal/day/week/month requests with 4 sections/day.
 - Ingredient mapping returns canonical IDs or explicit unmatched list.
 - Fallback generator can replace Gemini without breaking the demo path.
+
+## Immediate AI Focus
+
+- Freeze the JSON response schema before any model integration work.
+- Define ingredient matching against `usda_foods` because there is no standalone `ingredients` table.
+- Build fallback canned week plan early so the demo is never blocked on Gemini.

 - [ ] `P0` Assign session owners and communication cadence (check-ins at 2h/4h/6h/8h).
 - [ ] `P0` Create shared decision log with timestamps and owner.
 
 ### 1) Spec freeze for parallel work (0:30-2:00)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Freeze API v1 contract by 2h mark.
+- [x] `P0` Publish auth/profile API v1 docs for implemented endpoints.
+- [ ] `P0` Freeze pantry/plan/chat API v1 contract for remaining work.
 - [ ] `P0` Freeze AI response shape v1 (meals/day split, ingredient fields, macros, cost).
 - [ ] `P0` Freeze frontend MVP screens and navigation map.
 - [ ] `P0` Define acceptance rules for plan proposal (accept, decline/regenerate, favorite optional).
 
 ### 2) Integration management (2:00-6:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Resolve cross-team blockers in <=15 minutes each.
 - [ ] `P0` Verify backend/frontend contract alignment after every major endpoint.
 - [ ] `P0` Verify AI ingredient matching strategy and fallback behavior.
 - [ ] `P1` Keep release notes updated with what is demo-ready vs partially complete.
 
 ### 3) Demo readiness (6:30-8:00)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Run full walkthrough with QA observer.
 - [ ] `P0` Prepare demo script with exact clicks and API actions.
 - [ ] `P0` Mark risks and fallback moves for each step (especially AI outage).
 - [ ] `P0` Final go/no-go checklist with owners for each section.
 
 ## Contracts Needed From Others
 
 - Backend: stable endpoint list, payload examples, migration/setup command.
 - Frontend: final routes/screens and expected loading/error states.
 - AI: prompt template, JSON schema, fallback plan output format.
 - QA: final bug severity rubric and test checklist coverage.
 
 ## Risks
 
 - API contract drift after 2h causing rework.
 - AI output variability breaking parser.
 - Pantry deduction logic unclear at edge cases.
 - Time spent on optional features reducing demo stability.
 
 ## Done Criteria
 
 - API and AI contracts are frozen and shared.
 - End-to-end demo script passes at least once without manual DB edits.
 - Each session has explicit must-have vs nice-to-have status.
 - Fallback demo path exists if AI service fails.
+
+## Immediate PM Focus
+
+- Lock the remaining contract for pantry, plans, and chat before frontend starts real UI work.
+- Decide whether monthly plan generation is true P0 or just a thin extension of weekly flow.
+- Decide whether favorites stays P1 or is dropped for the hackathon demo.

13: type AuthHandler struct {
14: 	auth *services.AuthService
15: }
16: 
17: func NewAuthHandler(auth *services.AuthService) *AuthHandler {
18: 	return &AuthHandler{auth: auth}
19: }
20: 
21: func (h *AuthHandler) Register(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
22: 	var req dto.RegisterRequest
23: 	if err := ReadJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
24: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "bad_request", "invalid JSON payload")
25: 		return
26: 	}
27: 
28: 	res, err := h.auth.Register(r.Context(), req)
29: 	if err != nil {
30: 		switch {
31: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidEmail), errors.Is(err, services.ErrWeakPassword):
32: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "validation_error", err.Error())
33: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrEmailConflict):
34: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusConflict, "conflict", err.Error())
35: 		default:
36: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to register user")
37: 		}
38: 		return
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, res)
42: }
43: 
44: func (h *AuthHandler) Login(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
45: 	var req dto.LoginRequest
46: 	if err := ReadJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
47: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "bad_request", "invalid JSON payload")
48: 		return
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	res, err := h.auth.Login(r.Context(), req)
52: 	if err != nil {
53: 		switch {
54: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidCredentials):
55: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", err.Error())
56: 		default:
57: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to login")
58: 		}
59: 		return
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, res)
63: }
64: 
65: func (h *AuthHandler) Me(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
66: 	userID, ok := middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context())
67: 	if !ok {
68: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing auth context")
69: 		return
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	me, err := h.auth.Me(r.Context(), userID)
73: 	if err != nil {
74: 		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
75: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "user not found")
76: 			return
77: 		}
78: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to load current user")
79: 		return
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]dto.UserResponse{"user": me})
83: }

16:     weight_kg REAL,
17:     age INTEGER,
18:     sex TEXT,
19:     activity_level TEXT,
20:     goal TEXT,
21:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
22:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
23:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
24:     CHECK (sex IN ('female', 'male', 'other', 'prefer_not_to_say') OR sex IS NULL)
25: );
26: 
27: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_preferences (
28:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
29:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
30:     diet_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'omnivore',
31:     allergies_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
32:     dislikes_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
33:     likes_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
34:     daily_calorie_target INTEGER,
35:     notes TEXT,
36:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
37:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
38:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
39: );
40: 
41: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS budgets (
42:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
43:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
44:     month TEXT NOT NULL,
45:     currency TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD',
46:     amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (amount_cents >= 0),
47:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
48:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
49:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
50:     UNIQUE (user_id, month)
51: );
52: 
53: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS purchases (
54:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
55:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
56:     budget_id TEXT,
57:     fdc_id INTEGER,
58:     description TEXT,
59:     quantity REAL,
60:     unit TEXT,
61:     cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (cost_cents >= 0),
62:     purchased_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
63:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
64:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
65:     FOREIGN KEY (budget_id) REFERENCES budgets(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
66:     FOREIGN KEY (fdc_id) REFERENCES usda_foods(fdc_id) ON DELETE SET NULL
67: );
68: 
69: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipes (
70:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
71:     name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
72:     meal_type TEXT NOT NULL,
73:     servings INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (servings > 0),
74:     instructions TEXT,
75:     total_kcal REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
76:     total_protein_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
77:     total_carbs_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
78:     total_fat_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
79:     estimated_cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
80:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
81:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
82:     CHECK (meal_type IN ('breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner', 'snacks'))
83: );
84: 
85: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipe_ingredients (
86:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
87:     recipe_id TEXT NOT NULL,
88:     fdc_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
89:     quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity >= 0),

91:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
92:     FOREIGN KEY (recipe_id) REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
93:     FOREIGN KEY (fdc_id) REFERENCES usda_foods(fdc_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
94:     UNIQUE (recipe_id, fdc_id, unit)
95: );
96: 
97: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pantry_items (
98:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
99:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,

102:     unit TEXT NOT NULL,
103:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
104:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
105:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
106:     FOREIGN KEY (fdc_id) REFERENCES usda_foods(fdc_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
107:     UNIQUE (user_id, fdc_id, unit)
108: );
109: 
110: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS meal_plans (
111:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
112:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
113:     period_type TEXT NOT NULL,
114:     start_date DATE NOT NULL,
115:     end_date DATE NOT NULL,

119:     ai_cost_cents_total INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (ai_cost_cents_total >= 0),
120:     notes TEXT,
121:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
122:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
123:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
124:     CHECK (period_type IN ('day', 'week', 'month')),
125:     CHECK (status IN ('proposal', 'accepted', 'declined', 'archived')),
126:     CHECK (source IN ('ai', 'fallback', 'manual')),

129: 
130: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_meals (

1: # Backend API v1 - Auth and Profile
2: 
3: Base URL (local): `http://localhost:8080`
4: 
5: ## Error format
6: 
7: All failures return:
8: 
9: ```json
10: {
11:   "error": {
12:     "code": "validation_error",
13:     "message": "human readable message"
14:   }
15: }
16: ```
17: 
18: ## Authentication
19: 
20: - Token type: Bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
21: - Token expiry: controlled by `TOKEN_TTL_HOURS` (default `24`)
22: 
23: ## Endpoints
24: 
25: ### `POST /auth/register`
26: 
27: Request:
28: 
29: ```json
30: {
31:   "email": "newuser@pantrypal.local",
32:   "password": "Passw0rd!",
33:   "displayName": "New User"
34: }
35: ```
36: 
37: Response `201`:
38: 
39: ```json
40: {
41:   "token": "<bearer-token>",
42:   "expiresAt": "2026-05-29T12:00:00Z",
43:   "user": {
44:     "id": "usr_...",
45:     "email": "newuser@pantrypal.local",
46:     "displayName": "New User"
47:   }
48: }
49: ```
50: 
51: ### `POST /auth/login`
52: 
53: Request:
54: 
55: ```json
56: {
57:   "email": "newuser@pantrypal.local",
58:   "password": "Passw0rd!"
59: }
60: ```
61: 
62: Response `200`: same shape as register response.
63: 
64: ### `GET /me`
65: 
66: Headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
67: 
68: Response `200`:
69: 
70: ```json
71: {
72:   "user": {
73:     "id": "usr_...",
74:     "email": "newuser@pantrypal.local",
75:     "displayName": "New User"
76:   }
77: }
78: ```
79: 
80: ### `GET /profile`

82: Headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
83: 
84: Response `200`:
85: 
86: ```json
87: {
88:   "user": {
89:     "id": "usr_...",

108:   },
109:   "budget": {
110:     "month": "2026-06",
111:     "currency": "USD",

113:   }
114: }
115: ```
116: 
117: ### `PATCH /profile/metrics`
118: 
119: Headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
120: 
121: Request:
122: 
123: ```json
124: {
125:   "heightCm": 172,
126:   "weightKg": 70,
127:   "age": 29,
128:   "sex": "male",
129:   "activityLevel": "moderate",

134: Response `200`: full `GET /profile` shape.

1: # Backend DB Setup
2: 
3: Local SQLite setup for backend Phase 1 tasks.
4: 
5: Food source note:
6: - App pantry and recipe ingredients now reference USDA foods directly via `fdc_id`.
7: - The old local `ingredients` table is no longer part of the schema.
8: 
9: ## One-command full reset
10: 
11: Run from repo root:
12: 
13: ```bash
14: ./scripts/db/reset-local-db
15: ```
16: 
17: This will:
18: - Recreate `database/sqlite/pantrypal.db`
19: - Apply `backend/migrations/001_init_schema.sql`
20: - Apply `backend/migrations/010_usda_schema.sql`
21: - Seed demo data from `backend/seeds/001_seed_demo.sql`
22: - Import USDA dataset from `dataset/FoodData_Central_foundation_food_json_2026-04-30.json`
23: 
24: ## Optional commands
25: 
26: Create DB only if missing:
27: 
28: ```bash
29: ./scripts/db/local_db.sh setup
30: ```
31: 
32: Reset app tables only (preserve USDA tables and imported data):
33: 
34: ```bash
35: ./scripts/db/local_db.sh reset-app
36: ```
37: 
38: Force recreate DB (app + USDA):
39: 
40: ```bash
41: ./scripts/db/local_db.sh reset-all
42: ```
43: 
44: Alias kept for compatibility:
45: 
46: ```bash
47: ./scripts/db/local_db.sh reset
48: ```
49: 
50: Override DB file path:
51: 
52: ```bash
53: DB_PATH=/tmp/pantrypal-demo.db ./scripts/db/local_db.sh reset
54: ```
55: 
56: Disable USDA import on setup/reset-all:
57: 
58: ```bash
59: IMPORT_USDA=0 ./scripts/db/local_db.sh reset-all
60: ```
61: 
62: Use a different USDA dataset file path:
63: 
64: ```bash
65: USDA_DATASET_PATH=/tmp/fdc.json ./scripts/db/local_db.sh reset-all
66: ```

(End of file - total 66 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package services
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"errors"
7: 	"strings"
8: 
9: 	"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
10: 
11: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/auth"
12: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/repositories"
13: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
14: )
15: 
16: var (
17: 	ErrInvalidEmail       = errors.New("email is invalid")
18: 	ErrWeakPassword       = errors.New("password must be at least 8 characters")
19: 	ErrEmailConflict      = errors.New("email already registered")
20: 	ErrInvalidCredentials = errors.New("invalid credentials")
21: )
22: 
23: type AuthService struct {
24: 	users  *repositories.UserRepository
25: 	tokens *auth.TokenManager
26: }
27: 
28: func NewAuthService(users *repositories.UserRepository, tokens *auth.TokenManager) *AuthService {
29: 	return &AuthService{users: users, tokens: tokens}
30: }
31: 
32: func (s *AuthService) Register(ctx context.Context, req dto.RegisterRequest) (dto.AuthResponse, error) {
33: 	req.Email = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Email))
34: 	req.DisplayName = strings.TrimSpace(req.DisplayName)
35: 	if !strings.Contains(req.Email, "@") {
36: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrInvalidEmail
37: 	}
38: 	if len(req.Password) < 8 {
39: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrWeakPassword
40: 	}
41: 
42: 	exists, err := s.users.ExistsByEmail(ctx, req.Email)
43: 	if err != nil {
44: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
45: 	}
46: 	if exists {
47: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrEmailConflict
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(req.Password), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
51: 	if err != nil {
52: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	user, err := s.users.CreateUser(ctx, req.Email, string(hash), req.DisplayName)
56: 	if err != nil {
57: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	token, expiresAt, err := s.tokens.MakeToken(user.ID)
61: 	if err != nil {
62: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	return dto.AuthResponse{
66: 		Token:     token,
67: 		ExpiresAt: expiresAt.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00"),
68: 		User: dto.UserResponse{
69: 			ID:          user.ID,
70: 			Email:       user.Email,
71: 			DisplayName: user.DisplayName,
72: 		},
73: 	}, nil
74: }
75: 
76: func (s *AuthService) Login(ctx context.Context, req dto.LoginRequest) (dto.AuthResponse, error) {
77: 	req.Email = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Email))
78: 	user, err := s.users.GetByEmail(ctx, req.Email)
79: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
80: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrInvalidCredentials
81: 	}
82: 	if err != nil {
83: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
84: 	}
85: 	if bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(user.PasswordHash), []byte(req.Password)) != nil {
86: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrInvalidCredentials
87: 	}
88: 
89: 	if err := s.users.EnsureDefaultProfileRows(ctx, user.ID); err != nil {
90: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	token, expiresAt, err := s.tokens.MakeToken(user.ID)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	return dto.AuthResponse{
99: 		Token:     token,
100: 		ExpiresAt: expiresAt.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00"),
101: 		User: dto.UserResponse{
102: 			ID:          user.ID,
103: 			Email:       user.Email,
104: 			DisplayName: user.DisplayName,
105: 		},
106: 	}, nil
107: }
108: 
109: func (s *AuthService) Me(ctx context.Context, userID string) (dto.UserResponse, error) {
110: 	user, err := s.users.GetByID(ctx, userID)
111: 	if err != nil {
112: 		return dto.UserResponse{}, err
113: 	}
114: 	return dto.UserResponse{ID: user.ID, Email: user.Email, DisplayName: user.DisplayName}, nil
115: }

(End of file - total 115 lines)
</content>

2:

7: APP_MIGRATION_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/backend/migrations/001_init_schema.sql"
8: USDA_SCHEMA_MIGRATION_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/backend/migrations/010_usda_schema.sql"
9: SEED_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/backend/seeds/001_seed_demo.sql"
10: USDA_IMPORT_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/scripts/db/import_usda.py"
11: USDA_DATASET_PATH="${USDA_DATASET_PATH:-$ROOT_DIR/dataset/FoodData_Central_foundation_food_json_2026-04-30.json}"
12: IMPORT_USDA="${IMPORT_USDA:-1}"
13: MODE="${1:-reset-app}"
14: 
15: if [[ ! -f "$APP_MIGRATION_PATH" ]]; then
16:   printf "Missing migration file: %s\n" "$APP_MIGRATION_PATH" >&2
17:   exit 1
18: fi
19: 
20: if [[ ! -f "$USDA_SCHEMA_MIGRATION_PATH" ]]; then
21:   printf "Missing migration file: %s\n" "$USDA_SCHEMA_MIGRATION_PATH" >&2
22:   exit 1
23: fi
24: 
25: if [[ ! -f "$SEED_PATH" ]]; then
26:   printf "Missing seed file: %s\n" "$SEED_PATH" >&2
27:   exit 1
28: fi
29: 
30: mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DB_PATH")"
31: 
32: apply_migrations() {
33:   (

35:     go run ./cmd/dbseed -db "$DB_PATH" -migration "$APP_MIGRATION_PATH"
36:   )
37: 
38:   sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" < "$USDA_SCHEMA_MIGRATION_PATH"
39: }

43: }
44: 
45: usda_food_count() {

47:     printf "0"
48:     return
49:   fi

51:   sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM usda_foods;" 2>/dev/null || printf "0"
52: }
53: 
54: import_usda_if_configured() {

69:   python3 "$USDA_IMPORT_PATH" --db-path "$DB_PATH" --dataset-path "$USDA_DATASET_PATH"
70: }

77: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS consumption_log;

83: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS purchases;
84: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS budgets;
85: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS user_preferences;
86: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS user_body_metrics;
87: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS users;
88: 
89: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
90: SQL
91: }

95:     if [[ -f "$DB_PATH" ]]; then
96:       printf "Database already exists: %s\n" "$DB_PATH"
97:       printf "Use '%s reset-all' to recreate it.\n" "$0"
98:       exit 0
99:     fi
100:     apply_migrations
101:     import_usda_if_configured
102:     seed_demo
103:     ;;
104:   reset-app)

106:       reset_app_tables

109:     if [[ "$(usda_food_count)" == "0" ]]; then

121:     printf "Usage: %s [setup|reset-app|reset-all|reset]\n" "$0" >&2
122:     exit 1

3: import (
4: 	"flag"

8: 
9: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/db"
10: )
11: 
12: func main() {
13: 	dbPath := flag.String("db", "../database/sqlite/pantrypal.db", "path to sqlite db file")
14: 	migrationPath := flag.String("migration", "./migrations/001_init_schema.sql", "path to migration sql file")
15: 	seedPath := flag.String("seed", "", "optional path to seed sql file")
16: 	reset := flag.Bool("reset", false, "delete db file before applying migration + seed")
17: 	flag.Parse()
18: 
19: 	if *reset {
20: 		_ = os.Remove(*dbPath)
21: 	}
22: 
23: 	conn, err := db.Open(*dbPath)
24: 	if err != nil {
25: 		log.Fatalf("open db: %v", err)
26: 	}
27: 	defer conn.Close()
28: 
29: 	if err := db.ApplySQLFile(conn, *migrationPath); err != nil {
30: 		log.Fatal(err)
31: 	}
32: 	if strings.TrimSpace(*seedPath) != "" {

2: 
3: import (
4: 	"crypto/hmac"
5: 	"crypto/sha256"
6: 	"encoding/base64"
7: 	"encoding/json"
8: 	"errors"
9: 	"strings"
10: 	"time"
11: )
12: 
13: type Claims struct {
14: 	UserID string `json:"uid"`
15: 	Exp    int64  `json:"exp"`
16: }
17: 
18: type TokenManager struct {
19: 	secret []byte
20: 	ttl    time.Duration
21: }
22: 
23: func NewTokenManager(secret string, ttl time.Duration) *TokenManager {
24: 	return &TokenManager{secret: []byte(secret), ttl: ttl}
25: }
26: 
27: func (t *TokenManager) MakeToken(userID string) (string, time.Time, error) {
28: 	expiresAt := time.Now().UTC().Add(t.ttl)
29: 	claims := Claims{UserID: userID, Exp: expiresAt.Unix()}
30: 
31: 	payload, err := json.Marshal(claims)
32: 	if err != nil {
33: 		return "", time.Time{}, err
34: 	}
35: 	payload64 := base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(payload)
36: 	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, t.secret)
37: 	if _, err := mac.Write([]byte(payload64)); err != nil {
38: 		return "", time.Time{}, err
39: 	}
40: 	sig := base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
41: 	return payload64 + "." + sig, expiresAt, nil
42: }
43: 
44: func (t *TokenManager) ParseToken(token string) (Claims, error) {
45: 	parts := strings.Split(token, ".")
46: 	if len(parts) != 2 {
47: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("invalid token format")
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, t.secret)
51: 	if _, err := mac.Write([]byte(parts[0])); err != nil {
52: 		return Claims{}, err
53: 	}
54: 	expected := mac.Sum(nil)
55: 	actual, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[1])
56: 	if err != nil {
57: 		return Claims{}, err
58: 	}
59: 	if !hmac.Equal(expected, actual) {
60: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("token signature mismatch")
61: 	}
62: 
63: 	payload, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[0])
64: 	if err != nil {
65: 		return Claims{}, err
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	var claims Claims
69: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(payload, &claims); err != nil {
70: 		return Claims{}, err
71: 	}
72: 	if claims.UserID == "" || claims.Exp == 0 {
73: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("invalid token claims")
74: 	}
75: 	if time.Now().UTC().Unix() > claims.Exp {
76: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("token expired")
77: 	}
78: 	return claims, nil
79: }

**Updating task documentation**

I see I need to update the task documentation to mark what’s been implemented. I’ll take a closer look at the scripts, particularly run-backend.sh, and consider if the documentation should indicate a contract freeze. I want to be clear about what’s been implemented, especially regarding the health endpoint. It's essential to update the master and session files with status markers. I should also differentiate between completed and partial tasks, while noting any discrepancies in the schema references!

**Reviewing database schema and endpoints**

I've noticed the database schema has many future tables, but it lacks services and endpoints. I’ll need to inspect the router to see if it includes only auth, profile, and health endpoints, which might be enough. I think I should use apply_patch to update all task files. Marking completed items with [x] and partial items with [] makes sense, but I'll keep it simple. For partial items, I’ll clarify what’s done and what’s not, ensuring we don’t falsely mark tasks as complete!

**Updating status snapshots**

I think it’s a good idea to reword items as pending and add a status snapshot section to each file. This might be useful for clarity. I want to update the pm, backend, frontend, AI, and bugs sections and consider adding "Current Status" sections that detail completed artifacts. I'll ensure it's efficient and include summaries, mentioning there's no code beyond markdown. First, I’ll read the task files to edit, keeping in mind that there likely won't be any frontend files to work with. Let's patch the TASKS master significantly!

I’m updating the markdown boards now. I’m also correcting the ingredient/data wording to match the actual implementation, which uses USDA tables plus demo recipe/pantry seeds instead of a standalone `ingredients` table.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /root/Panathenaia/pantryPal/tasks/pm.md
@@
 ## Objective
 
 - Keep the 8-hour demo focused on a working end-to-end flow.
 - Lock scope quickly, unblock cross-session dependencies, and manage risk/time.
+
+## Current Status Snapshot
+
+- Backend Phase 1 is partially complete: auth/profile foundation, DB schema, seed data, and setup docs are implemented.
+- Frontend, AI, and QA workstreams are still open.
@@
 ### 1) Spec freeze for parallel work (0:30-2:00)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Freeze API v1 contract by 2h mark.
-- [ ] `P0` Freeze AI response shape v1 (meals/day split, ingredient fields, macros, cost).
+- [x] `P0` Publish auth/profile API v1 docs for implemented endpoints.
+- [ ] `P0` Freeze pantry/plan/chat API v1 contract for remaining work.
+- [ ] `P0` Freeze AI response shape v1 (meals/day split, ingredient fields, macros, cost).
 - [ ] `P0` Freeze frontend MVP screens and navigation map.
 - [ ] `P0` Define acceptance rules for plan proposal (accept, decline/regenerate, favorite optional).
@@
 ## Done Criteria
 
 - API and AI contracts are frozen and shared.
 - End-to-end demo script passes at least once without manual DB edits.
 - Each session has explicit must-have vs nice-to-have status.
 - Fallback demo path exists if AI service fails.
+
+## Immediate PM Focus
+
+- Lock the remaining contract for pantry, plans, and chat before frontend starts real UI work.
+- Decide whether monthly plan generation is true P0 or just a thin extension of weekly flow.
+- Decide whether favorites stays P1 or is dropped for the hackathon demo.
*** End Patch

+- Consumption endpoint plus pantry deduction and logging.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /root/Panathenaia/pantryPal/tasks/frontend.md
@@
 ## Objective
 
 - Build a clean, fast demo UI that proves all core flows using the backend API.
+
+## Current Status Snapshot
+
+- Frontend has folder structure only.
+- No HTML, JS, CSS, routing, state, or API integration is implemented yet.
+- Auth/profile backend endpoints already exist and are ready to consume.
@@
 ### 1) App shell and API client (0:30-1:30)
 
 - [ ] `P0` Set up page structure and shared layout (auth, profile, planner, pantry, chat).
 - [ ] `P0` Build API client helpers for auth headers, JSON handling, and error display.
 - [ ] `P0` Add simple route/state switching (no framework) between major sections.
@@
 ## Done Criteria
 
 - User can authenticate, edit profile, generate/accept a weekly plan, and consume a meal.
 - Planner clearly shows 7 days x 4 sections with macro totals.
 - Pantry updates reflect manual edits and automatic meal consumption.
 - Chat actions are usable via buttons without manual API calls.
+
+## Immediate Frontend Focus
+
+- Build auth and profile screens first because the API already exists.
+- Delay weekly planner and pantry UI until backend contracts for plans/pantry are frozen.
+- Keep the first usable milestone as: login/register -> fetch profile -> edit profile.
*** End Patch
